title: Charmed Life - Contents (Fragment 1)
text:
Contents

Contents

Note: T HERE ARE T HOUSANDS of worlds, all different from ours.

1 C AT C HANT A DMIRED his elder sister Gwendolen. She was a witch.

2 C AT WAS A LARMED by Miss Larkins. She was the daughter of Mr. Larkins at the junk shop.

3 T HE T RAIN J OURNEY lasted about an hour, before the train puffed into Bowbridge. . .

4 T HE S AME S OFTNESS and silence were there when the red-haired Mary woke Cat. . .

5 G WENDOLEN R EFUSED TO tell Cat what she was going to do.

6 G WENDOLEN G AVE V ENT to her fury in her room after dinner.

7 I T R AINED ON Saturday afternoon. Gwendolen shut herself into her room. . .

8 G WENDOLEN W ENT D OWN to the village to get her dragons’ blood on Wednesday afternoon.

9 B Y T HIS T IME , Cat was as bewildered as the strange girl seemed to be. Chant?

10 C AT W ATCHED H IS moment and, when Mr. Saunders called them to lessons. . .

11 B Y THE T IME Janet had laced both her boots, Cat was sure it was lunchtime.

12 C HRESTOMANCI’S S TUDY WAS a large, sun-filled room with books in shelves. . .

13 T HEY WERE U SHERED into a private parlor in the White Hart.

14 C AT S CREAMED . Flames burst out of him all over. He screamed again. . .

15 I T WAS A G LORIOUS start-of-autumn day, with everything green and gold, hot and still.

16 “C AT,” SAID C HRESTOMANCI , from almost behind Cat’s head. “Cat!”

vocabulary.definition: to feel confused or perplexed
confused or puzzled completely
vocabulary.example: Cat was as bewildered as the strange girl seemed to be.

vocabulary.definition: a rare red resin used in magic and medicine
older or senior in age or rank
intense, uncontrollable anger
vocabulary.example: Gwendolen gave vent to her fury.

vocabulary.definition: to express strong feelings
having great beauty and splendor
vocabulary.example: It was a glorious start-of-autumn day.

vocabulary.definition: an act of travelling from one place to another
vocabulary.example: Train journey lasted about an hour.

translations.ru: лоток с хламом, скупка ненужных вещей

vocabulary.definition: a store selling cheap or used items
vocabulary.example: Junk shop owned by Mr. Larkins.

vocabulary.definition: to fasten shoes or boots by tying laces
a very short period of time
a small, private sitting room
to emit smoke or steam in short bursts
vocabulary.example: The train puffed into the station.

vocabulary.definition: worlds that are connected or associated
to make a loud, high-pitched sound, usually from pain or fear
to close something or keep oneself inside
vocabulary.example: Gwendolen shut herself into her room.

vocabulary.definition: complete absence of sound
referring to the early period of autumn season
to escort or show someone to their place
vocabulary.example: They were ushered into a private parlor.

vocabulary.definition: a woman believed to have magical powers
